I would like the fish, please.
Can I have the bill, please?

I would like the bill, please.
Can I have the menu, please?
Good afternoon. Here is the menu. Are you ready to order?
Yes, thank you. I would like the fish dish, please.
Good choice. And a drink?
Can I have a water, please? And the dessert menu later.
Of course. I will bring the food now.
Thank you. And later, can I have the bill, please?

Good afternoon. Welcome to our restaurant. Here is the menu. Today we have a special dish: grilled fish with rice. For a drink, we have water, juice, and coffee. Would you like a starter first? Our soup is very good. And for dessert, we have fruit and chocolate cake. Please take your time. I will come back to take your order. Thank you.

Today I had a business lunch with a client. I was a little nervous, but it was easy. I looked at the menu. I said, I would like the fish, please. For a drink, I asked for water. The waiter was very kind. At the end, I asked for the bill. The client said, your English is good, Anna. Now I feel confident to order food in English. A business lunch is not difficult.

First, Anna arrives at the restaurant and the waiter brings the menu. Then she looks at the dishes and the drinks. Next, she says, I would like the fish, please. After that, the waiter brings the food and the drink. Finally, Anna asks for the bill and says thank you.
